entry.title: Re: Billing Document Reprint for Different Parties
entry.content:
Hello,

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud public edition, you can reprint billing documents and customize the output to display different headings for each copy intended for various parties. To achieve this, follow these general steps:

1. **Copy and Customize the Standard Form Template:**
- Begin by copying a standard billing document form template that closely matches your requirements.
- Customize this copied template to include specific headings such as "Original for Recipient", "Duplicate for Transporter", "Triplicate for Supplier", and "Customer Acknowledgement".

2. **Utilize Output Control:**
- Configure the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Output Control settings to manage the printing sequences and customize which headings appear on each copy.
- Ensure that in your configuration, you specify the conditions or rules that determine which template is printed for each party.

3. **Create Multiple Output Types:**
- In your configuration, you might also define different output types for each version of the document if required.
- Assign different output types or assignments to these forms to distinguish between types of reprints.

4. **Watermark for Duplicates:**
- Make use of the duplicate printing functionality to include distinct identifiers or watermarks on the copied forms if needed. This will help you ensure compliance and clarity for each recipient.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Unlock S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: Why Key Users are Crucial in Moving from Business ByDesign
entry.content:
Introduction

Transitioning from SAP Business ByDesign to S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is more than a technical upgrade; it requires a strategic shift and a true Cloud Mindset. This blog post draws from our key user real-world experiences and their learnings and feedback, showing how the success of this journey hinges on the drive of these critical team members.

How to Select Key Users

  • Embrace Best Practices: S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is about adopting SAP’s best practices, so don’t limit your search to Businsess ByDesign experts, find individuals who understand the limitations of the Business ByDesign, the reasons for the move and are genuinely committed to embracing the Cloud Mindset and its inherent best practices.
  • Understand the “Why”: Select individuals who understand the underlying business needs not just the business process as it is implemented, they need to be able to articulate the why behind their work.
  • Strong Communicators: Key users are the bridge between the project team and end-users, they must be able to clearly explain the benefits of adopting new business processes and address any concerns.

Where are Key Users vital in transition?

  • Championing Change: Involved from the start, key users leverage their understanding of both Business ByDesign and S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (see “Training and Support” below) to effectively communicate the changes and benefits to their teams. They can bridge the gap by relating new processes to familiar Business ByDesign concepts, making them powerful advocates for change.
  • Business Process Review: During the Fit-to-Standard workshops in the Explore phase, key users play a crucial role in evaluating S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition's capabilities against current Business ByDesign processes. They champion the adoption of standard S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition processes, helping determine the optimal configurations and identify any genuine gaps.
  • Training and Support: As the first line of training and support, key users leverage their dual-system experience to anticipate user questions and tailor training effectively. They are often designated as Support or Influence key users, liaising directly with SAP and need comprehensive training on the SAP Activate methodology and relevant business process content, available on SAP Learning.
  • Beyond implementation: The most valuable feedback we receive comes from key users. Their feedback and insights and along with those gathered from the Influence Request Portal, are used to improve implementation processes and help shape future product releases.

Key User involvement from the start 

We can already see the significant involvement required of the key users and consequently the demands placed upon them, this is not without a price and gives rise to several challenges.

The Challenges of Key User Involvement

  • Time Commitment & Overburdening Key Users: Key users, often already stretched due to their expertise, are now tasked with learning a new system, participating in workshops and supporting their teams. Underestimating this time commitment is a major pitfall and a common reason for project delays. They have the potential to significantly influence and optimise business processes, but being time poor can mean missed opportunities. We’ve seen several delays in go-lives directly attributable to both key user availability and resistance to change, the next point.
  • Resistance to Change: Change is inherently disruptive. Some key users may resist S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition best practice due to a perceived loss of control, especially if they were deeply involved in customising Business ByDesign in the past. They may fear that their expertise will be undermined or be wary due to negative experiences with past implementations, so this resistance needs to be addressed proactively for a successful transition.

Overcoming the Challenges

  • Address Resistance and Involve Key Users Early: Acknowledge potential resistance and address it head-on, importantly review past implementation projects, to understand the root cause of any friction and identify lessons learned. This allows you to tailor the S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition implementation to avoid repeating past mistakes., Build trust and demonstrate that you are listening to concerns, SAP’s Activate methodology recommends key-user involvement from the Discover phase onwards and this early engagement allows them to contribute to requirements gathering and process design. We had an instance where it was the customer’s Project Manager resisting key user early involvement, including them only from the fit-to-standard workshops, aiming to avoid consuming too much of their time. This backfired and this key part of the Explore phase became derailed as key users scrambled to understand the reasons scope items were selected (or not) and even why the transition itself was the best option.
  • Proper Selection and Training: Select key users with strong communication skills, a deep understanding of business needs and a willingness to embrace and evangelise change. We have also seen great success where 2-3 users are appointed per process, with one in the lead, this not only helps lighten the load, but also a valuable forum for discussion.  The key users were also selected from different but related departments, resulting in a fresh perspective, challenging existing processes and mindset and seeing some truly innovative ideas. Successful transition projects are where we see time and budget allocated to train key users using SAP Activate’s accelerators and in particular learning journeys on SAP Learning.

 Conclusion

Key users are vital to a successful transition from Business ByDesign to S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. When you invest in their selection, training, and support, you're not just implementing software; you're building a team that can truly own the change.

What are your experiences with key user involvement in implementations? Share your thoughts in the comments below. And if you'd like to learn more about how our customers have successfully transitioned please see my other blog and also hear from customers themselves in this podcasts.

Please also see my other blog: Key Success Factors for Transition from SAP Business ByDesign  to S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Episode 103: SAP Customer Transitioning Experience: SAP Business ByDesign to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Publi...

Episode 111: Transitioning from SAP Business ByDesign to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: After Go-...

entry.title: SAP EWM: sequencing picking WTs for multiple Stock Types in single WO
entry.content:
I am encountering an issue with a scenario where a single wave contains items with two different stock types (e.g., F1 and F6) originating from two separate storage locations. These stock types are derived from two distinct Outbound Delivery Orders, which have items merged into one wave.

Additionally, the items from these ODOs have been planned to be consolidated into a single PSHU using transaction code /SCWM/CAP. After releasing the wave, a Warehouse Order is created containing all items from both ODOs. However, the tasks associated with the second stock type appear at the end of the sequence of warehouse tasks, which disrupts the expected pick path consistency.

Is there a way, within standard configuration, to ensure that all picking tasks are sequenced according to the pick path, regardless of stock type?

entry.title: EWM Form & Label Print through PPF
entry.content:
Introduction

EWM uses the Post Processing Framework (PPF) to specify whether PPF is able to perform a PPF action (for example, printing), within the PPF, EWM calls determination of the condition technique in order to find the appropriate condition records for performing a PPF action.

In this post, I would like to introduce how to set up Forms & Labels print in EWM. This blog will not go in detail how to create Form or create custom PPF but about assign Form and setup condition record to trigger Form/Label printing. Example is based on Standard form.

This post includes below Form/Label:

1. Warehouse Order/ Warehouse Task print

2. Handling Unit print

3. TU Loading list print

4. Physical Inventory count sheet print

5. Delivery Note print

Prerequisites

Defined warehouse-number-specific print settings (in particular the spool parameters).

Tcode: /SCWM/60000431 - Maintain Warehouse-Specific Printing Parameters

Create spool and assign default printer

Print Warehouse Order/ Warehouse Task

EWM can use this function to automatically print lists during creation of warehouse orders and warehouse tasks. These lists contain information about the storage bin to and from which a warehouse employee should move specific goods.

From warehouse management monitor, can manually print a warehouse order, which also prints all corresponding warehouse tasks. For example, the first printout is damaged, or your require a second printout.

Example scenarios to trigger WO Print:

  • Putaway label - WO Single - Contents HU number, Product, quantity, source bin, destination bin,...ect
  • Picking list - WO Multiple - Contents WT list of 1 WO, Product, pick quantity, source HU, source bin...ect

Example Standard WO Smart Form 

Prerequisites

IMG: SCM Extended Warehouse Management > Extended Warehouse Management > Cross-Process Settings > Warehouse Order > Print

Define Condition Types

Define Determination Procedure and assign Condition Type to Determination Procedure

Assign Determination Procedure to Warehouse and WPT

IMG: SCM Extended Warehouse Management > Extended Warehouse Management > Cross-Process Settings > Warehouse Order > Print > Define PPF Action Profiles

"Inactive" must not be ticked.

Create Condition record, Tcode: /SCWM/PRWO6 - Link Form with action definition

Condition is set to trigger PPF action, different form/ label can be set based on combination of WO status (open, confirmed, cancelled, blocked, in process), Activity Area, WPT and to be printed in different printer.

When WO/WT create or confirmed (depends on WO status set in condition), postprocessing framework calls the /SCWM/WME application with action definition /SCWM/WO and checks for the condition record for print action definition.

Example: Standard form /SCWM/WO_SINGLE

Example Standard form /SCWM/WO_MULTIPLE

 

 

Print Handling Unit

EWM can use this function to automatically print lists or labels according to your Customizing settings, when you work with handling units (HUs). The documents can contain information about the contents, destination, owner, product number, quantity, batch number, weight, volume and data necessary for further processing of the HU.

You can use this function in the warehouse management monitor manually perform or repeat printing for a HU.

Example scenarios to trigger HU label:

  • Creating HUs in work centers before putaway
  • Repacking HUs in work centers
  • Creating shipping labels for HUs before loading

Example Standard HU Form

Prerequisites

IMG: SCM Extended Warehouse Management > Extended Warehouse Management > Cross-Process Settings > Handling Units > Basics > Print

Define Condition Types

Define Determination Procedure & Assign Condition Type to Determination Procedure

IMG: SCM Extended Warehouse Management > Extended Warehouse Management > Cross-Process Settings > Handling Units > Basics > Print > Define PPF Action Profiles

Condition record, Tcode: /SCWM/PRHU6 - Link Form & Action Definition

Different HU label can be set based on combination of HU type, HU step (create, change, delete, manual trigger HU printing), packaging material.

When you save HUs or confirm a warehouse task, the system calls the PPF and starts an attempt to print. The decision whether to print is dependent on the condition records that are found in the schedule condition.

Example Standard form /SCWM/HU_SHPLABEL

Example Standard form /SCWM/HU_CONTENT

Print TU Loading List

In shipping and receiving, you can print the following documents for a vehicle or a transportation unit (TU) using PDF-based forms or SAP Smart Forms forms. This part will show example about TU Loading List.

In the header area of a TU or vehicle in the warehouse management monitor, you can manually print a TU-related document or vehicle-related document.

Example Standard Form

Prerequisites

IMG: SCM Extended Warehouse Management > Extended Warehouse Management > Cross-Process Settings > Shipping and Receiving > Message Processing > Define Action Profiles for Transportation Units

Tcode: /SCWM/SR_PRINT - Set Up Printer and Form Determination

Create a Print Profile, then assign Action definition + Printer to Print Profile

Define Access Sequence and assign Print profile + Form name 

Example Standard from /SCWM/SF_SR_TULOADLIST

Print Physical Inventory count sheet

The PI counting list includes PI counting items for the counting process. It also contains the warehouse order and PI document number as a bar code on header level.

The PI counting list contains the following information by default: Storage bin, Handling unit, Product, Product description, Batch, Quantity (for input), Unit of measure...

Form template /SCWM/PI_CNT_FIORI for landscape orientation or /SCWM/PI_CNT_FIORI_POR for portrait orientation.

Prerequisites

IMG: Extended Warehouse Management > Internal Warehouse Processes > Physical Inventory > Print

Define Condition Types

Create and Change Determination Procedure

Specify Action Profiles for Printing Physical Inv. Documents

Tcode: /SCWM/PRPI_GCM - Create Condition Records for Printing (Physical Inventory) 

Link Form & Action Definition

Print by Tcode: /SCWM/PI_PROCESS 

or Tcode: /SCWM/PRPI_DOC

Example standard form /SCWM/PI_CNT_DOC

Delivery Note print

EWM automatically prints the delivery note for an outbound delivery as soon as you have posted the goods issue. By default, EWM uses the predefined SAP Smart Form /SCWM/DLV_NOTE.

Prerequisites

IMG: Extended Warehouse Management > Cross-Process Settings > Delivery - Warehouse Request > Actions > Change Actions and Conditions Define Conditions

Define Action Profiles and Actions

IMG: Extended Warehouse Management > Goods Issue Process > Outbound Delivery > Print

Define Print Profiles for Delivery Processing

Define Print Profile Determination for Goods Issue Process

Determine Form Determination for Delivery Note Printing

Tcode: /n/SCWM/DLVPPFC

Example Standard Delivery Note

Reference

Integration of PPF and the Condition Technique 

How to use PPF in EWM

SAP HELP document for EWM PPF Printing

***

Thank you for your reading. I would like to receive any comment/question regarding my post.

entry.title: Re: ASA 17.0.10: Calling an external HTTP:GET service
entry.content:
Two things I would do is set the type to HTTPS (presumably the endpoint is using https?) and also set the protocol version to 1.1 (it defaults to 1.0), like this:

CREATE PROCEDURE "dba"."WebTestRun"(in "cAccessToken" long varchar )
result( "cAttribute" long varchar,"cValue" long varchar,"npp" integer )
url 'http://OtherSite/WebTest1'
set 'HTTP(EXCEPTIONS=off; VERSION=1.1)'
type 'HTTPS:GET'
header 'Authorization: !cAccessToken'

I suspect the real winner is Version 1.1 is important if the other side is proxying the connection as it requires a Host Header, 1.0 doesn't. That said I always force type's to HTTPS when I know the server uses it.


You can more about the various options here CREATE PROCEDURE Statement [Web Service] | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Custom Field in My Inbox Purchase Order Screen.
entry.content:
We are using the standard "My Inbox" Fiori application for managing purchase orders in SAP S/4HANA.

 

We have created a custom field that has been successfully added to the screen layout of the purchase order.

 

How can this custom field be displayed in My Inbox detail section ?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Custom Field in My Inbox Purchase Order Screen.
entry.content:
Dear customer 

this is documented on the bellow link:

 

https://help.sap.com/doc/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/2408.500/en-US/564e116c50a845478b97f0d31a591214.html?forSiteMap=true

 

The Display Purchase Order app is the source for the purchase order details shown in the My Inbox application and cannot be displayed independently of the My Inbox app. The Display Purchase Order application cannot be extended in the My Inbox  application. To extend the Display Purchase Order app view in the My Inbox application, proceed as follows:


1. You are a key user and a business role is assigned to your user, for example: SAP_BR_EXTENSIBILITY_SPEC to work with the Custom Tiles and the Custom Catalog Extensions apps

2. Create a tile for the Display Purchase Order application

3. Adapt the UI of the the Display Purchase Order application


Thanks SAP Technical Support

entry.title: Re: Mass changes in BOM items – (Changing the item quanti...
entry.content:
In S/4HANA Cloud we have the mass maintenance app 'Mass Maintenance of Bill of Material Items'

 

Information on the app is available at the resource Mass Maintenance of Bill of Material Items

The app is available when the business catalog SAP_PLM_BC_MBOM_MASS_PC is assigned to the User

The business catalog is available with the activation of the scope item 64F

 

If activating the scope item 64F is not possible , the alternative is to use the SAP API API_BILL_OF_MATERIAL_SRV_0002

entry.title: Dos and Donts to report on compensation data of employees with multiple pay components
entry.content:
Introduction

Typically, employees have multiple pay components and there is often a requirement for a report to show the amounts ​​of these pay components in different columns. This means that the requirement is that an employee should appear in a single row of a report output and, for example, the first column should report the amount of the base salary, the second column should report the amount of another pay component, and so on. If an employee does not receive a certain pay component, the amount of this entry should be zero.

Figure 1 – Example of an employee with 5 pay components

Figure 1 shows as an example the employee “Rachana Ahmad” with 5 pay components. The aim would therefore be for a report to report “Rachana Ahmad” in a single row and the various pay components in 5 or more columns.

A frequently used report design is illustrated in Figure 2. The objects “Compensation” and “Pay Component” are joined multiple times in the underlying query, with a filter being defined for exactly one pay component. Very poor report performance was observed on several occasions, particularly when up to 20 joins of “Compensation” => “Pay Component” were used in production instances. This is not least because in this case the permission provider is called separately.

Figure 2 – Non-performing query with 5 joins of „Compensation“ => „Pay Component“

The output is of course in the desired format (see Figure 3). However, this design should be avoided, especially because the identical output can easily be achieved by using very simplified queries.

Figure 3 – Report output of the non-performing query from Figure 2

This simplified query, as illustrated in Figure 4, uses a single instance of "Compensation" => "Pay Component".

Figure 4 – Simplified query with single join of „Compensation“ => „Pay Component“

Option 1

First of all, such a query naturally returns one row for each combination of employee and pay component (Figure 5).

Figure 5 – Report Output of the simplified query

However, as you see in Figure 6 if you use the table type “Cross-Tab” and use “Pay Component” as a column, you get an output that is very similar to the report output in Figure 3.

Figure 6 – Report output as „Cross-Tab“ with „Pay Component“ as Column

Option 2

Another option is to define a calculated column for each pay component based on the simplified query (Fig. 4), each of which contains the value of an individual pay component (Fig. 7). If, for example, you want to have 5 columns for the 5 pay components, you would have to define 5 calculated columns accordingly. 

Figure 7 – Calculated Column to derive the amount of each individual pay component

Figure 8 illustrates the report output. You can see that the report output is very similar to the output of option 1.

Figure 8 – Report output based on the example with 5 calculated columns

Option 3

Another option is to display the employees in one table and the pay components in a second table. These two tables are linked using "Link Analysis" so that by clicking on a row in the upper table, the lower table displays the pay components of the selected employee (Figure 9). This design also has the advantage that the upper table displays the respective number of the employee's pay components. It would also be possible to display the total sum of the accumulated pay components in a simple way in the upper table.

Figure 9 – Report output of 2 tables, connected via „Link Analysis“

entry.title: Functional Usage - Value Drivers and Peer Comparison
entry.content:
You know already the Functional Usage History section displaying the Usage Trend based on the Solution Capabilities defined by each product, see this blog.

Now we additionally released a new subsection "Business Value (Last Month)"  focusing on the last completed month only and providing additional functional usage details:

  • It displays to which extend the functionalities of your consumed SAP Public Cloud Products are used in the last completed month,  
  • it highlights functionalities represented by Solution Capabilities you are already using and provides insights into their related Value Drivers,
  • it shows to you the usage behavior of the relevant peer group from the same industry for comparison.

First graph “Solution Capabilities by Value Drivers”.

A Value Driver defines the general direction of improvement an organization can achieve in a specific business process. Solution Capabilities are contributing to the Value Drivers they are assigned to. Ideally all Solution Capabilities should be assigned to one or several Value Drivers. For some products this assignment might still be in progress.

Please note that only top 10 Value drivers with the most used Solution Capabilities are displayed as default.

For each Value Driver you see a horizontal bar with two categories:

  • number of Used Solution Capabilities (Count Distinct Solution Capability ID where Solution Capability Usage Status equals ‘Used’) reflected as a green segment and
  • number of Non-Used Solution Capabilities (Count Distinct Solution Capability ID where Solution Capability Usage Status equals ‘Non-Used’) reflected as a grey segment.

This chart supports you in identifying most affected Value Drivers as well as the Solution Capabilities you are not yet using but which are contributing to an important Values Driver. With that, added value can be discovered.

Second graph “Solution Capabilities by Usage Intensity”.

Here you can check to which intense are you using the product functionalities represented by Solution Capabilities and compare your usage with your industry peers for the last completed month.

Usage Intensity is determined by traffic values. There can be traffic of calls, documents or users. The traffic values of the considered "Traffic Type" are being compared to the traffic median of your industry peers to categorize your "Usage Intensity" per Solution Capability. The intensity categories are defined as follows:

  • High: “your traffic” = > (“Industry Traffic Median” + 10%)
  • Moderate: (“Industry Traffic Median” -10%) < “your traffic” < (“Industry Traffic Median" + 10%”)
  • Low: “your traffic” = < (“Industry Traffic Median" -10%”)
  • No intensity calculated: this refers to Solution Capabilities whose assigned Features are measured exclusively via the “users” metric.  Since "users" cannot be aggregated, usage intensity cannot be calculated on Solution Capability Level.

This chart supports you in visualizing your usage intensity compared with the peer group and in finding new usage values potential.

In the example below you can see that 3 Solution Capabilities from Commerce Solution Area are used by you more intensive that your peer group (green segment), 2 of them are used less intensive (red segment) and 7 of 12 Solution Capabilities are not used at all (grey segment). By clicking on grey segment with “7” you can see which business values you are missing (1st graph) and the details to the selected Solution Capabilities shown in the table.

Third graph “Solution Capabilities by Industry Peer”

This chart provides an overview of how many Solution Capabilities per Solution Area are used by a high, moderate or low number of customers of your industry peer group, reflected as an Industry Footprint category.

An Industry Footprint category is determined by the share of your industry peers using a Solution Capability in comparison to the Industry Footprint median of the linked Logical Product.

With this chart you can quickly identify Solution Capabilities you are not yet using but which are used by a high/moderate or low portion of your industry peers. You also immediately see which Solution Capabilities are used by a high number of customers (High footprint) and check if they are also used on your side and with which intensity.

In the example below you can select the 6 Solution Capabilities used by a high number of customers in your peer group and check how they are used in your organization. In this example you see that 4 of 6 Solution Capabilities are not used at all. By clicking on this section with 4 you can see in the table the details, navigate to the documentation of those non-used Solution Capabilities and check if they could be also used in your organization. You also see the related business value for them in the first chart, which you miss.

All Solution Capabilities in the 2nd and 3rd graph are grouped by Solution Areas. This makes easy to see for which product area the functional usage is good and where it can be improved. Additionally, it helps to compare your functional usage with the usage of the relevant peer group.  

By selecting a segment with the non-used Solution Capabilities in the 2nd graph you can check how they are used by the customers from your industry peer group (3rd graph). By clicking on the grey segment with “7” in example below (Solution Area Commerce) you see that from 7 Solution Capabilities non-used by you 4 are used by a high number of customers in your industry peer (3rd graph). If you click on the green segment “4” in the 3rd graph you can see the details for those Solution Capabilities in the table and check if they also could be used by you.  

Selecting a segment of a graph you can view details to the relevant Solution Capabilities in the table and in the other graphs. You also can use the filters on the top (Value Driver, Logical Product) to focus on a special group of Solution Capabilities.

The table below the graphs displays the details to the selected Solution Capabilities and from there you can navigate to the documentation or to the related product.

Please note that:

  • If your S-user is authorized for several accounts (corporate structure), consider that the usage intensity can be different for different accounts, which leads to the fact that the second graph can display more Solution Capabilities than the 3rd graph, even if you selected a segment.
  •  Due to the complex usage calculation the month-switch has 10 days delay. It means, it is only possible to see the values of the last completed month earliest on the 10th of the next month. This happens due to the complex usage calculation which needs more time to be finalized when a new month comes to its end.
  • A Solution Capability describes a functional ability of a single or multiple software products that implement and support a business capability (see documentation in the table link). Each Solution Capability is assigned at least to one logical product. Each logical product is assigned to at least one solution area.
  • The regular data updates for this section happen weekly. In the '...' menu you always can see the latest update status.

SAP for Me is made for you!

Stay updated by subscribing to this blog and visiting the SAP Support Portal / Customer Insights.

Related blogs: SAP for Me goes reporting  or Functional Cloud Usage (history) now live in SAP for Me and all related blogs to ‘Customer Insights’ reporting.

entry.title: Re: Sales Condition Contract Manual settlement document s...
entry.content:
Hi @RafiqSAPSD, 

I would recommend you to review the configuration guide - CCM_Sales_Rebates_Standard_Configuration - provided by SAP in the KBA 2535889 - CCM - Description of delivered configuration. 
Each condition types and pricing schema used for SAP CCM is described and explained.

Here the following explanation for REA5 and RENT: 
"Condition type REA5 Rebate AccrualsSettl has the same parameter values as REA2. You use this condition type in a settlement run for the amount of the accruals index update, see pricing procedures A10005 and A10007 in chapter Define Pricing Procedures. All parameter values are identical with values for REA2. So, note the Accruals checkbox in the Control Data 2 group box. It’s a manual condition type without access sequence because the condition amount is determined by the settlement program. To control the transfer to the pricing procedures in the settlement run, you specify this condition type in a condition contract type (see chapter Specify Settlement Settings for Condition Contract Types)."

"Explanation RENT: Not shown parameters have an initial value. You use this condition type for the business volume in manually created settlement documents, see pricing procedure A10008 in chapter Define Pricing Procedures."

Regards, 
Gauthier Victor

entry.title: Enabling Authorization Policies in SAP Cloud Identity Services - IAS with AMS/CAP
entry.content:
SAP Cloud Identity Services provide a robust framework for managing user identities and authorizations across various SAP applications. One of the key components of this framework is the Identity Authentication Service (IAS), which integrates seamlessly with the Authorization Management Service (AMS) and the Cloud Application Programming (CAP) model. This blog will guide you through the steps to enable authorization policies using these services.

Understanding the Components

1. Identity Authentication Service (IAS): IAS is a cloud service that provides secure authentication for users accessing SAP applications. It supports various authentication methods, including single sign-on (SSO), and integrates with other identity providers.

2. Authorization Management Service (AMS): AMS is a centralized service for managing authorizations across SAP applications. It allows administrators to define and enforce authorization policies, ensuring that users have the appropriate access rights.

3. Cloud Application Programming (CAP) Model: CAP is a framework for building enterprise-grade applications on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). It simplifies the development process by providing a set of tools and libraries for building, deploying, and managing applications.

Steps to Enable Authorization Policies

  1. Access SAP IAS Admin Console:

    • Log in to the SAP IAS Admin console through the SAP BTP cockpit - SAP Cloud Identity Services.
    • Navigate to the “Application & Resources” and "Tenant Settings" section.
    • Follow the Screenshot 1

  2. Enable Policy Based Authorization:

    • Click on Policy Based Authorizations and enable toggle button to activate the services
    • Follow the Screenshot 2

  3. Validate Authorization Policies visible:

    • Navigate back to "Applications" and Search for "Admin"
    • Under "System Applications" - Click on Administration Console
    • Check you will see additional tab - "Authorization Policies"
    • Follow the Screenshot 3

Screenshot 1

Screenshot 2

Screenshot 3

Step 3: Integrate AMS with CAP Applications

  1. Add AMS Dependencies:

    • In your CAP project, add the necessary AMS dependencies to your package.json or pom.xml file. For example:
      JSON
      "dependencies": {
  "@sap/ams-dev": "^2.0.0",
  "@sap/cds-dk": "^8.6.0"
}

  2. Configure Security in CAP:

    • Update your CAP application’s security configuration to integrate with AMS. This involves setting up JWT decoders and security filters to handle authorization tokens.
      Java
       
      @Configuration
@EnableWebSecurity
@Profile("cloud")
@Order(1)
public class SecurityConfig {
    @Autowired
    private JwtDecoder jwtDecoder;

    @Bean
    public SecurityFilterChain securityFilterChain(HttpSecurity http) throws Exception {
        http.authorizeRequests()
            .antMatchers("/public/**").permitAll()
            .anyRequest().authenticated()
            .and()
            .oauth2ResourceServer()
            .jwt()
            .decoder(jwtDecoder);
        return http.build();
    }
}

  3. Deploy and Test:

    • Deploy your CAP application to SAP BTP and test the authorization policies. Ensure that users can only access the resources they are authorized to


Conclusion

By following these steps, you can effectively enable and manage authorization policies in SAP Cloud Identity Services using IAS, AMS, and the CAP model. This setup ensures secure and efficient access control across your SAP applications, enhancing both security and user experience.

Feel free to reach out if you have any questions or need further assistance with your SAP Cloud Identity Services setup!

I hope this blog helps you get started with enabling authorization policies in SAP Cloud Identity Services. If you have any specific questions or need further details, let me know!

entry.title: Application Jobs in On-Premise systems - part3
entry.content:
For further study and own fun.

1. ADT to create class with Implementing the business logic.

 

Sample Code

CLASS zcl_test_apj_exe DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC .

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES if_apj_dt_exec_object.
    INTERFACES if_apj_rt_exec_object.
  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.


CLASS zcl_test_apj_exe IMPLEMENTATION.
 --input parameters
  METHOD if_apj_dt_exec_object~get_parameters.

ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD if_apj_rt_exec_object~execute.

ENDMETHOD.

-- Getting the actual parameter values
-- Implement the job execution

ENDCLASS.
 CLASS zcl_apj_demo_class DEFINITION PUBLIC FINAL CREATE PUBLIC .

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES if_apj_rt_run.
    INTERFACES if_apj_dt_defaults.

    TYPES:
      BEGIN OF ty_name_range,
        sign   TYPE c LENGTH 1,
        option TYPE c LENGTH 2,
        low    TYPE c LENGTH 50,
        high   TYPE c LENGTH 50,
      END OF ty_name_range.
    TYPES: ty_name_ranges TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_name_range WITH EMPTY KEY.

    "! <p class="shorttext synchronized" lang="en">Numbers available</p>
    DATA numbers_available TYPE abap_bool VALUE abap_true.
    "! <p class="shorttext synchronized" lang="en">Numbers</p>
    DATA numbers TYPE RANGE OF i.
    "! <p class="shorttext synchronized" lang="en">Names available</p>
    DATA names_available TYPE abap_bool VALUE abap_true.
    "! <p class="shorttext synchronized" lang="en">Names</p>
    DATA names TYPE ty_name_ranges.
    "! <p class="shorttext synchronized" lang="en">Some text</p>
    DATA text TYPE c LENGTH 255.
    "! <p class="shorttext synchronized" lang="en">Public attribute which is not used on the selection screen</p>
    DATA another_public_attribute TYPE c LENGTH 10.
ENDCLASS.

 

2. ADT to create job catalog entry as SAPJ/SE80 is no longer be used any more;

    Define header, section, group, parameters in ADT.

 

--Header Check class sample
CLASS zcl_test_apj_chk DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC .

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES if_apj_jt_check_20.
ENDCLASS.


CLASS zcl_test_apj_chk IMPLEMENTATION.
METHOD if_apj_jt_check_20~check_and_adjust.
ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

 

More Methods:

 

--Define potential search help in parameters

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED

@ObjectModel.dataCategory: #VALUE_HELP

@EndUserText.label: 'Test Plant CDS view'

@Search.searchable: true

define view entity zcl_test_apj_table 
as select from zcl_apj_table {...}

 

3. ADT to create job template as SAPJ/SE80 is no longer be used any more;

 

Sample Code
--https://help.sap.com/docs/ABAP_PLATFORM_NEW/b5670aaaa2364a29935f40b16499972d/1f04ad22db0147b99ebc476708b749b6.html?version=202310.002

CLASS zcl_test_apj_simple_obj_gen DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC .

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES if_oo_adt_classrun.
  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.


CLASS zcl_test_apj_simple_obj_gen IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD if_oo_adt_classrun~main.

    CONSTANTS lc_catalog_name      TYPE cl_apj_dt_create_content=>ty_catalog_name  VALUE 'ZTEST_MY_SIMPLE_JOB'.
    CONSTANTS lc_catalog_text      TYPE cl_apj_dt_create_content=>ty_text          VALUE 'My first simple application job'.
    CONSTANTS lc_class_name        TYPE cl_apj_dt_create_content=>ty_class_name    VALUE 'ZCL_TEST_APJ_SIMPLE'.

    CONSTANTS lc_template_name     TYPE cl_apj_dt_create_content=>ty_template_name VALUE 'ZTEST_MY_SIMPLE_JOB_TEMPL'.
    CONSTANTS lc_template_text     TYPE cl_apj_dt_create_content=>ty_text          VALUE 'My first simple job template'.

    CONSTANTS lc_transport_request TYPE cl_apj_dt_create_content=>ty_transport_request VALUE 'Y11K900361'.
    CONSTANTS lc_package           TYPE cl_apj_dt_create_content=>ty_package           VALUE 'Z_D028092_APJ_MAIN'.

    DATA(lo_dt) = cl_apj_dt_create_content=>get_instance( ).

    " Create job catalog entry (corresponds to the former report incl. selection parameters)
    " Provided implementation class iv_class_name shall implement two interfaces:
    " - if_apj_dt_exec_object to provide the definition of all supported selection parameters of the job
    "   (corresponds to the former report selection parameters) and to provide the actual default values
    " - if_apj_rt_exec_object to implement the job execution
    TRY.
        lo_dt->create_job_cat_entry(
            iv_catalog_name       = lc_catalog_name
            iv_class_name         = lc_class_name
            iv_text               = lc_catalog_text
            iv_catalog_entry_type = cl_apj_dt_create_content=>class_based
            iv_transport_request  = lc_transport_request
            iv_package            = lc_package
        ).
        out->write( |Job catalog entry created successfully| ).

      CATCH cx_apj_dt_content INTO DATA(lx_apj_dt_content).
        out->write( |Creation of job catalog entry failed: { lx_apj_dt_content->get_text( ) }| ).
    ENDTRY.

    " Create job template (corresponds to the former system selection variant) which is mandatory
    " to select the job later on in the Fiori app to schedule the job
    DATA lt_parameters TYPE if_apj_dt_exec_object=>tt_templ_val.

    NEW zcl_test_apj_simple( )->if_apj_dt_exec_object~get_parameters(
      IMPORTING
        et_parameter_val = lt_parameters
    ).

    TRY.
        lo_dt->create_job_template_entry(
            iv_template_name     = lc_template_name
            iv_catalog_name      = lc_catalog_name
            iv_text              = lc_template_text
            it_parameters        = lt_parameters
            iv_transport_request = lc_transport_request
            iv_package           = lc_package
        ).
        out->write( |Job template created successfully| ).

      CATCH cx_apj_dt_content INTO lx_apj_dt_content.
        out->write( |Creation of job template failed: { lx_apj_dt_content->get_text( ) }| ).
        RETURN.
    ENDTRY.

  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

 

4. Setup authorizations 

Find out how to set up the authorizations for the Application Jobs app.

The authorizations for application jobs are the same as for classic batch jobs if you use the default concept. For more information, see 101146 Information published on SAP site.

You can switch to the new authorization concept for application jobs by following the description in 3190922 Information published on SAP site.

5. Maintain application jobs via a released API

   You can use the CL_APJ_RT_API class to maintain application jobs. You can do the following and more:

  • schedule an application job

  • retrieve the status of an application job

  • cancel an application job

  • delete an application job

6. Maintain application job logs

 

Sample Code
CLASS zcl_test_write DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC .

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES if_oo_adt_classrun.
  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.


CLASS zcl_test_write IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD if_oo_adt_classrun~main.

    TRY.
        " Create a new Application Log
        DATA(l_log) = cl_bali_log=>create( ).

        " Add a header to the log
        l_log->set_header( header = cl_bali_header_setter=>create( object = 'ZOBJECT'
                                                                   subobject = 'ZSUBOBJECT'
                                                                   external_id = 'External ID' ) ).

        " Add a message as item to the log
        DATA(l_message) = cl_bali_message_setter=>create( severity = if_bali_constants=>c_severity_error
                                                          id = 'PO'
                                                          number = '000' ).
        l_log->add_item( item = l_message ).

        " Add a second message, this time from system fields SY-MSGID, ...
        MESSAGE ID 'ZTEST' TYPE 'S' NUMBER '058' INTO DATA(l_text).

        l_log->add_item( item = cl_bali_message_setter=>create_from_sy( ) ).

        " Add a free text to the log

        DATA(l_free_text) = cl_bali_free_text_setter=>create( severity = if_bali_constants=>c_severity_error
                                                              text = 'Some Error Text' ).

        l_log->add_item( item = l_free_text ).

        " Add an exception to the log
        DATA: i TYPE i.
        TRY.
            i = 1 / 0.
          CATCH cx_sy_zerodivide INTO DATA(l_ref).
        ENDTRY.

        DATA(l_exception) = cl_bali_exception_setter=>create( severity = if_bali_constants=>c_severity_error
                                                              exception = l_ref ).
        l_log->add_item( item = l_exception ).

        " Save the log into the database
        cl_bali_log_db=>get_instance( )->save_log( log = l_log ).

      CATCH cx_bali_runtime INTO DATA(l_runtime_exception).
        out->write( l_runtime_exception->get_text(  ) ).
    ENDTRY.

  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

 

7. Notification and exceptions

 

CLASS zcl_ntf DEFINITION PUBLIC FINAL CREATE PUBLIC.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES IF_APJ_RT_JOB_NOTIF_EXIT.
ENDCLASS.


CLASS zcl_ntf IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD if_apj_rt_job_notif_exit~notify_jt_start.
  ENDMETHOD.


  METHOD if_apj_rt_job_notif_exit~notify_jt_end.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

 

8. Define your own APP with report, setup sematic objects, mapping target etc.

 

Some others interesting ones:

What is the scope of the Application Job App F1240?
In Detail it was asked: Is it meant to show all the jobs which could be seen in SM37 or is there a limitation to the jobs which it can see?

Yes there is a limitation. As the name indicates the Fiori App F1240 will only display jobs of the type "Application Job" it will not display any other type of jobs. Therefore NO - F1240 will not show the same information like SM37. One major aspect for this is that F1240 will only depict the application jobs that are allowed to be seen by cloud customers and partners and will hide all SAP proprietary jobs which must not be exposed to cloud customers. SM37 by its architecture and nature can NOT differentiate such jobs and therefore will always show every job - event those that are SAP confidential and proprietary. Therefore this view will not be exposed in any cloud environment.

After adding Application "(F1204) Application Jobs" to a custom business role I cannot schedule my Application Job. Is this an error?
NO, most likely this behaviour is the correct and the expected one. F1240 is by default a READ-ONLY application that will only display all jobs of type "Application Job" scheduled in the current client by ALL users. By architecture the application does not deliver any S_START authorizations and therefore by default the scheduling of any application job is not possible. The application does offer a restriction type via which the customer administrator can maintain enhanced authorizations which allow the abortion of any application job of any user in the system. Example: maintaining restrictions with an asterisk "*" the Business User that will receive this particular customer Business Role will be allowed to abort every application job of ever business user in the system.

If you wonder, why in your particular system you just access for your testing/development this same application is assigned to a Business User - but this user is able to also create and schedule new application jobs, than the reasoning is in all likelihood the following:

if to a Business Role arbitrary other Business Catalogs / App Authorizations Variants are assigned the authorizations of these artefacts are also merged into the role
even though F1240 does not provide any S_START authorization → any other application F???? can contain S_START authorization
the user is able to schedule the jobs, because he received valid S_START authorizations via any arbitrary F????
I am working in a cloud environment (e.g. S/4 HANA Public Cloud, IBP, Steampunk...) and when I open SM37 I can see more jobs compared to application F1240. Is this an error?
NO. In cloud environments the access for customers and partners to particular content in transaction SM37 is prohibited and must not be shared with customer and partner users. SM37 is capable to display classic background jobs ([BC-SRV-BTC] SM36/ JOB_OPEN JOB_CLOSE interfaces ...), Technical Jobs ([BC-SRV-BTC-JR] SJOBREPO - strictly prohibited for external exposure, SAP proprietary jobs) and Application Jobs ([BC-SRV-APS-APJ] Reuse-UIs; F1240; external communication scenarios ...). These are three diverse frameworks with divers user groups and diverse use cases demanding diverse feature sets (even when they share some technological assets and the same DB persistence). Therefore the access to SM37 is solely granted to SAP employees and solely via SAP Support scenarios as classic SAP GUI transaction access.

I have scheduled a job via the interfaces JOB_OPEN / JOB_CLOSE and alike, but they do not show up in Fiori Application F1240 - Application Jobs. Is this an error?
NO. You have used the interfaces to schedule classic background jobs and not an application job. Classic Jobs are to be monitored in the transaction SM37. F1240 does only show Application Jobs and not classic background jobs.

Can I use any existing API which works on Background Jobs also with Application Jobs? - Example: BP_JOB_COPY / BP_JOB_CREATE / BP_JOB_MODIFY
It is not allowed to use any API that was designed for classic background processing (SM36/SM37) for application jobs (nor technical jobs). Application Jobs and Technical Jobs are designed as superior layer above the classic background processing and therefore are only allowed to be accessed via dedicated released APIs that take the new and independent framework specifics into account. Using other APIs can lead to error situations which are not covered by our support and development teams and can only be solved by removing such erroneous calls.

And funny questions:

How to delete application jobs in transaction SM37?
Check SAP Note: 3002849.
To enable this, the setting to delete Application Jobs must be activated in the system by using the report BTC_OPTIONS_SET, Option: Delete Application Jobs from SM37.Note: In SM37 application jobs appear under their technical job names. You can retrieve the technical name of an application job from the Job Scheduling Fiori App by adding the column 'Job ID' to the job overview.

 

 

 

Useful SAP Notes and links:

SAP Note 2829891 - Application Jobs in On Premise systems;

SAP Note 3373331 - Application Jobs in OnPremise - FAQ;

SAP Note 3441346 - Enable 'Maintain Job Users' app for OnPremise

SAP Note 3438433 - Show technical users in the 'Maintain Job Users' app

SAP Note 3002849 - Application jobs cannot be deleted in SM37

SAP Note 2832889 - Application jobs must not be changed in SM36 or SM37 Message no. BT895

Creating a Job Catalog Entry and a Job Template in ADT

Working with Application Job Catalog Entries | SAP Help Portal

ABAP Cloud Application Jobs: Overcoming Key Challe... - SAP Community

Classes and Interfaces of the Application Log API | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Application Jobs in On-Premise systems - part2
entry.content:
In 3442285 - Handling of hidden and read-only parameters in the Application Jobs framework

Hidden Parameters

Hidden parameters need to be handled in a special way. First it is not allowed to maintain any parameter values inside a Job Template. The reason is, that customers might create their own templates, either via Scheduling or via Template App, and there is no 'copy mechanism' for hidden parameters. Consequently the customer's template would not have any value for those hidden parameter. Secondly parameters values must be set in the special way:

if your check exit class implements old check exit interface IF_APJ_JT_CHECK the one and only place to maintain values for hidden parameters is CHECK_BEFORE_SCHEDULE method.
if your check exit class implements new check exit interface IF_APJ_JT_CHECK_20 the one and only place to maintain values for hidden parameters is ADJUST_HIDDEN method.
Read-only Parameters

Whatever holds for Hidden Parameters will also hold for Read-Only Parameters: The idea behind read-only parameters is that users are not able to change them. Even if they are displayed when selecting the corresponding template, they are neither send back from the UI (as this request could be manipulated) nor could they be set by the framework.

if your check exit class implements old check exit interface IF_APJ_JT_CHECK the only places to maintain values for read-only parameters are INITIALIZE.
if your check exit class implements new check exit interface IF_APJ_JT_CHECK_20 the one and only place to maintain values for read-only parameters is ADJUST_READ_ONLY method.

Solution

We recommend using the new check exit interface IF_APJ_JT_CHECK_20 as handling such parameters is easier.

It is also possible to use the GET_DYNAMIC_PROPERTIES method, which allows a parameter to be dynamically hidden or set to read-only. !! IMPORTANT!! Parameters marked as read-only or hidden in SAPJ cannot be made visible or editable using this method.

Now let us take one example to feel this from the pre-deliver job catalog entries - SAP_FIN_ICA_MATCHING_RUN_COMP which runs against on ABAP reports: 1. ICA_MATCHING_RUN_AFCA for accounting model(leading unit is RCOMP).

In SAPJ - Job Catalog Entry

Header

Parameters: P_AFC marked as hidden.

In SAPJ - Job Template Name

The standard report run as below and you can get that "Trigger from AFC" was ticked as default in the report.

The ABAP report has the following parameters:

  1. Matching Method (Mandatory);
  2. Fiscal Year:  It's part of Filter String in ICARM.  It's related to the Mandatory Filter Fields FISCYEARPER.
  3. Fiscal Period: It's part of Filter String in ICARM.  It's related to the Mandatory Filter Fields FISCYEARPER.
  4. Company:  It's part of Filter String in ICARM.  It's related to the leading unit.
  5. Data Cutoff (YYYYMMDDhhmmss); 
  6. Safety Interval (Seconds);
  7. Trigger Follow-Ups;
  8. Trigger from AFC; A technical field. Hidden in job template and AFC.

The check class CL_ICA_APJT_CHECK_COMPANY defined as:

In the definition of method: IF_APJ_JT_CHECK_20~ADJUST_HIDDEN, P_AFC will be hidden during job scheduling with the exit class.

Compared to the standard interface method:

After the authorization and permission assigned to your users:

Launch to the APP Schedule Matching Run Jobs By Company and then Choose Create to create a job based on the job template “Matching Run – By Company”.

Choose Step 2 Scheduling Options:

You can select the Start Immediately check box to trigger an immediate job run, or deselect it to define a future start date and time for a single run, or define the recurrence pattern for the jobs you want to run regularly.

Choose Step Parameters:
Select the matching Method you want to use, and optionally, enter Fiscal Year, Fiscal Period, Company, Data Cutoff and Safety Interval.
If you want the Follow-Ups to be triggered, you can tick the option ‘Trigger Follow-UPs’. And here you can see the "Trigger from AFC" as it was hidden in class method above.

After scheduling and job was completely, when you check the parameters, you will get from there that "Trigger from AFC" was hidden but actually send to job variables.

The end.

When you define your own job catalog entry and check exit class method, you can take this as a reference.

Thanks

-Eric

entry.title: Field "Capacity Allocation Number" in Work Center Migration Object
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support,

I'm facing an issue with the Migration Object "Work center/Resource" in the Migration Cockpit: this template is requesting a field called "Capacity Allocation Number" as mandatory for the work center, but this field does not exist in our current system configuration. Additionally, this field isn't relevant to our plant maintenance module, as it's not required for our operations.

Our question then is:

  • How do we proceed without using this field in the data migration template? Or how do we resolve this scenario?

Thank you for your assistance,
Data migration experts


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Field "Capacity Allocation Number" in Work Center Mig...
entry.content:
Dear experts,

The fields set as mandatory in the template files of Migration Objects, like "Capacity Allocation Number" in "Work center/Resource", are done so when they are at least:

  • Set as mandatory according to SAP's Best Practices; or
  • In the application BAPI used in the migration, the field is set as mandatory.

This information is documented in the following KBA of the knowledge base:

  • 2700145 - Migration Cockpit: Why a field is set as mandatory or what are the requirements of the mandatory fields?

As a possible workaround, if your business context won't use such mandatory field at all, you could check the SAP Fiori Library for possible mass processing apps or interfaces for the business object mentioned.

Otherwise, to use the Migration Object, the mandatory sheets and data must be filled for the migration to work.

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Sales Condition Contract Manual settlement document s...
entry.content:
Hi Rafiq,

In Manual settlement document, RENT condition type is used for the settlement amount. The settlement amount entered in the initial screen will be copied into this condition type. The same amount will be copied to REA5 condition type with negative sign. REA5 in general is used to post the accruals to settlement index table WCOCOF from settlement document. In case of manual rebate settlement document, REA5 condition type will be used to reverse accruals for the manual settlement. Hence you will be seeing the REA5 with negative value in the manual rebate settlement document and in WCOCOF it will be posted with positive value.

In WB2R_SC, you might have seen negative value for any of the below two reasons.

a. You did the manual settlement of the condition contract and then went for the WB2R_SC for the same contract. In manual settlement you might have settled more than the eligible amount. example: Based on the business volume, customer is eligible for $5000 as rebate. You have settled $6000 through manual settlement. Now if you go with WB2R_SC, you will see -$1000.

b. As Gauthier Victor said, check if there any returns or credit memos exist in the business volume. Returns and Credits will reduce the rebate amount.

Below screenshots is from manual rebate settlement document. ZRA5 has negative sign.

Accruals will be posted to WCOCOF table with +sign(reduces the accruals by the settlement amount).

Hope this helps.

Regards

Hari

entry.title: Simple method to wrap the subfield access for X and XSTRING variables
entry.content:
The SUBSTRING function works only on CLIKE variables (C, D, N, T and STRING), not on X and XSTRING variables.

For X and XSTRING variables, the only solution is the subfield access DATAOBJECT+OFF(LEN).

This construct however doesn't allow using an expression for any of the three arguments (data object, offset or length).

For instance, I'd like a function SUBSTRING_X which allows this writing:

 

IF start = substring_x( val = xstring_table[ 1 ]
                        off = 0
                        len = xstrlen( start ) ).
  ...

 

So, here is the solution:

 

CLASS lcl_app DEFINITION.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    CLASS-METHODS substring_x
      IMPORTING val           TYPE xsequence
                !off          TYPE i DEFAULT 0
                !len          TYPE i DEFAULT -1
      RETURNING VALUE(result) TYPE xstring
      RAISING   cx_sy_range_out_of_bounds.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS lcl_app IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD substring_x.
    IF len = -1.
      result = val+off.
    ELSE.
      result = val.
      result = result+off(len).
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

 

Thanks for reading this short post.

Sandra

Stephan

entry.title: Re: Doubt for optimize abap code
entry.content:
This is code of ArcherZhang in the format recommed by Sandra_Rossi

 

DATA: lt_sobid TYPE TABLE OF hrp1001-sobid, lt_posids TYPE TABLE OF hrp1001-objid. 

" Collect all position IDs first 

LOOP AT is_ee_org_ass_repl_req-org_assignment INTO DATA(ls_org_assignment).
 APPEND ls_org_assignment-position_id TO lt_posids.
ENDLOOP.
 " Ensure the table is not empty to prevent runtime errors

  IF lt_posids IS NOT INITIAL.
   " Fetch all relevant sobids in a single SELECT statement 
   SELECT sobid FROM hrp1001 CLIENT SPECIFIED 
      INTO TABLE lt_sobid FOR ALL ENTRIES IN lt_posids
       WHERE mandt = @SY-mandt  AND 
             plvar = '01'       AND 
             objid = @LT_posids AND 
             begda <= lv_fecha_final AND
             endda >= lv_fecha_final AND
             subty = 'A012'.
   ENDIF. 

   " Processing the results as needed 
   LOOP AT lt_sobid INTO DATA(ls_sobid). 
   " Your logic here using ls_sobid 
   ENDLOOP.
    " Key changes: 
    " Use of FOR ALL ENTRIES: This minimizes the number of database calls by collecting all position IDs first.
    " Batch processing: Fetch results using a single database operation.
     "Defensive programming: Check if the table carrying data to fetch (lt_posids) is not initial.
    " Make sure to adjust the final logic loop as per your requirement with the lt_sobid results.
    " This approach reduces database load significantly and should improve performance.

entry.title: My ABAP in Eclipse wish list for 2025 enhancements / new features
entry.content:
• "Run in Projekt" Funktion. 
    • We often develop in client 100 but run / test in client 120. So it would be great if we could set a "run in client" in which Unit Tests / Run / F9 etc are executed.  So connect 120 as the "run in" client to development client 100.

  • Allow Comments in RAP Service Definition files.
  • Create Methods using method chains:  
    • currently  ZCL_Factory=>get_instance( )->createNewMethodWithWizzard()    is not supported 

  • Move multiple ABAP Classes / Objects into a new package ( currently is only one possible )

  • Allow method call as parameters for functions modules instead of only variables. #ABAPCore
  • Auto Refresh test client on debug start.  We are adding new lines in client 100. Than we run test in client 120, but the code is not refreshed, we have to refresh the code in the debugger manually to see the cursor on the correct lines.

  • Debugger, compare two structures in a comfortable view.  Example old and new data.

  • Allow method (getter) call in where clause    #ABAPCore

  • Refactoring: add push-down method (additionally to pull-up method ; or is is called pull-down) - with selection of subclass.  

  • Show CDS abapdoc comments (/**...*/) in Element View (just like abapdoc in classes)  [including @link might work automatically]
  • Markdown in abapdoc (or other structuring features - <ul><li></li> is very cumbersome; but also headings, bold, external links, etc.)

  • Fix abapdoc export, improve and add other elements e.g. ddic, CDS Views ; add adt links; initial page, navigation

  • Method signature (shift-Enter) provide functional signature
    • data(matnr) = get_matnr( vbeln = vbeln posnr = posnr ) instead of
    • get_matnr( exporting vbeln = vbeln posnr = posnr returning matnr = matnr ) 

 

 

Former post of feature Requests  :

https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-blog-posts/adt-feature-requests-after-2-years-of-use-in-project/ba-p/13375168
